text: The company reported a 25% increase in quarterly revenue, exceeding analyst expectations by a significant margin.
Operating profit fell to EUR 35.4 million from EUR 68.1 million in the corresponding period last year.
The board of directors will propose a dividend of EUR 0.12 per share for the fiscal year 2023.
Nokia announced the acquisition of Alcatel-Lucent, creating one of the largest telecommunications equipment manufacturers globally.
The company's market share in Europe declined from 15.3% to 12.8% over the past twelve months.
Net sales for the third quarter totaled EUR 1.2 billion, compared with EUR 1.1 billion in the same period last year.
The company issued a profit warning, citing unexpected supply chain disruptions and rising raw material costs.
Annual general meeting will be held on March 15, 2024, at the company headquarters in Helsinki.
The strategic restructuring plan is expected to generate annual savings of approximately EUR 50 million starting from 2025.
Credit rating agency Moody's downgraded the company's long-term debt rating from Baa1 to Baa2 with a negative outlook.
